session.user:
You are performing a bulk rename of JSON output field names in Python exec scripts.
IMPORTANT: Only rename STRING KEYS in dicts (like `"plan_number"`) and docstring JSON examples. Do NOT rename: - Python variable names (like `plan_number = ...`) - Click argument/option names (like `@click.argument("plan_number")`) - Function parameter names
For each file, read it first, then use Edit to replace `"plan_number"` with `"pr_number"` ONLY in dict literal keys and docstring JSON examples.
Process these files (all under /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/):
1. `plan_update.py` - line 168: `"plan_number": plan_number,` → `"pr_number": plan_number,` 2. `detect_plan_from_branch.py` - lines 12, 20, 53: rename `"plan_number"` in docstring examples and return dict 3. `objective_save_to_issue.py` - lines 155, 265: rename `"plan_number"` in dicts 4. `plan_save.py` - lines 347, 441: rename `"plan_number"` in dicts 5. `post_workflow_started_comment.py` - line 36: rename `"plan_number"` in docstring example 6. `setup_impl_from_pr.py` - lines 19, 210, 256, 297: rename `"plan_number"` in docstring and dicts (but NOT line 315 which is a click argument) 7. `close_pr.py` - line 76: rename `"plan_number"` in dict (but NOT line 25 which is a click argument) 8. `add_plan_label.py` - line 59: rename `"plan_number"` in dict (but NOT line 23 which is a click argument)
Read each file, then apply edits. Be precise with the old_string to ensure uniqueness.

1→"""Update an existing plan's content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-update --plan-number N [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command updates the plan content comment on an existing GitHub issue: 7→1. Find plan file (from session scratch, --plan-path, or ~/.claude/plans/) 8→2. Update plan content via PlanBackend 9→3. Update issue title from plan H1 heading 10→ 11→Options: 12→ --plan-number N: Plan number to update (required) 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage 14→ --plan-path PATH: Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup) 15→ 16→Output: 17→ --format json (default): {"success": true, ...} 18→ --format display: Formatted text 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Success - plan comment updated 22→ 1: Error - plan not found, no plan found, etc. 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk_shared.context.helpers import (
... [151 lines omitted] ...
37→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 83→ def _handle_update_error(error_msg: str, cause: Exception | None = None) -> None: 84→ """Output error message and exit with code 1.""" 86→ click.echo(f"Error: {error_msg}", err=True) 88→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": error_msg})) 106→ _handle_update_error("No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/") 115→ _handle_update_error(f"Plan #{plan_number} not found") 123→ except RuntimeError as e: 124→ _handle_update_error(f"Failed to update comment: {e}", cause=e) 133→ except RuntimeError as e: 134→ _handle_update_error(f"Failed to update title: {e}", cause=e) 152→ branch_updated = not isinstance(push_result, PushError) 162→ click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")

1→"""Detect plan number from the current git branch. 2→ 3→Uses github.get_pr_for_branch() to look up the associated PR for plnd/ branches. 4→Plan-ref.json is the primary source; this exec script is the fallback when no 5→.erk/impl-context/ folder exists yet. 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ erk exec detect-plan-from-branch 9→ 10→Output: 11→ JSON with detection result: 12→ {"found": true, "plan_number": 2521, "detection_method": "pr_lookup"} 13→ {"found": false} 14→ 15→Exit Codes: 16→ 0: Always (caller decides how to handle not-found) 17→ 18→Examples: 19→ $ erk exec detect-plan-from-branch 20→ {"found": true, "plan_number": 2521, "detection_method": "pr_lookup"} 21→""" 22→ 23→import json 24→from collections.abc import Callable 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_cwd, require_git, require_github, require_repo_root 29→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 30→
... [62 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Save plan as objective GitHub issue. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec objective-save-to-issue [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command extracts a plan and creates a GitHub issue with: 7→- erk-objective label only (NOT erk-plan - objectives are not plans) 8→- No title suffix 9→- Plan content directly in body (no metadata block) 10→- No commands section 11→ 12→Options: 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 14→ --format: json (default) or display 15→ --validate: Run objective validation after creation 16→ 17→Exit Codes: 18→ 0: Success - objective issue created 19→ 1: Error - no plan found, gh failure, etc. 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→from pathlib import Path 24→from typing import Any, cast 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk.cli.commands.objective.check_cmd import ( 29→ ObjectiveValidationError, 30→ ObjectiveValidationSuccess,
... [247 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Plan save: creates a draft PR with the plan content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-save [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→Options: 7→ --format json|display: Output format (default: json) 8→ --plan-file PATH: Use specific plan file (highest priority) 9→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 10→ --plan-type standard|learn: Plan type (default: standard) 11→ --learned-from-issue INT: Parent plan (for learn plans) 12→ --created-from-workflow-run-url URL: Workflow run URL 13→ 14→Objective linking: use --objective to pass directly, or rely on 15→automatic linking via the objective-context session marker 16→(created by /erk:objective-plan). The --objective flag takes 17→precedence over the session marker. 18→ 19→Exit Codes: 20→ 0: Success 21→ 1: Error 22→ 2: Validation failed 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.validate_plan_content import _validate_plan_content

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Post a "workflow started" comment to a GitHub issue with YAML metadata block. 3→ 4→This command posts a structured comment to a GitHub issue indicating that a 5→GitHub Actions workflow has started. The comment includes a YAML metadata block 6→that can be parsed programmatically. 7→ 8→This replaces ~40 lines of bash heredoc template assembly in GitHub Actions workflows. 9→ 10→Usage: 11→ erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \\ 12→ --plan-number 123 \\ 13→ --branch-name my-feature-branch \\ 14→ --pr-number 456 \\ 15→ --run-id 12345678 \\ 16→ --run-url https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/12345678 \\ 17→ --repository owner/repo 18→ 19→Output: 20→ JSON object with success status 21→ 22→Exit Codes: 23→ 0: Success (comment posted) 24→ 1: Error (GitHub API failed) 25→ 26→Examples: 27→ $ erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \\ 28→ --plan-number 123 \\ 29→ --branch-name feat-auth \\ 30→ --pr-number 456 \\
... [151 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Set up .erk/impl-context/ folder from GitHub PR in current worktree. 2→ 3→This exec command fetches a plan from a GitHub PR, creates a feature branch, 4→checks it out, and creates the .erk/impl-context/ folder structure for implementation. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec setup-impl-from-pr <plan-number> [--session-id <id>] 8→ 9→Output: 10→ Structured JSON output with success status and folder details 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success (.erk/impl-context/ folder created, branch checked out) 14→ 1: Error (PR not found, plan fetch failed, git operations failed) 15→ 16→Examples: 17→ $ erk exec setup-impl-from-pr 1028 18→ {"success": true, "impl_path": "/path/to/.erk/impl-context", 19→ "plan_number": 1028, "branch": "P1028-..."} 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→from pathlib import Path 24→ 25→import click 26→ 27→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 28→ require_branch_manager, 29→ require_cwd, 30→ require_git,

## Summary
I've successfully completed the bulk rename of JSON output field names in all 8 Python exec scripts. Here are the changes made:
